case.title: TAHITI BAR, INC., v. PENNSYLVANIA LIQUOR CONTROL BOARD

opinion.h1: TAHITI BAR, INC., v. PENNSYLVANIA LIQUOR CONTROL BOARD, 361 U.S. 85 (1959)
opinion.h4: TAHITI BAR, INC., v. PENNSYLVANIA LIQUOR CONTROL BOARD ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 363. Decided November 9, 1959.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.

Anne X. Alpern, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, Lois G. Forer, Deputy Attorney General, George G. Lindsay, Assistant Attorney General, and Russell C. Wismer, Special Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: LEHIGH CASINO, v. PENNSYLVANIA LIQUOR CONTROL BOARD, 361 U.S. 85 (1959)
opinion.p: 361 U.S. 85 LEHIGH CASINO, INC., v. PENNSYLVANIA LIQUOR CONTROL BOARD ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 379. Decided November 9, 1959.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.

Anne X. Alpern, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, Lois G. Forer, Deputy Attorney General, George G. Lindsay, Assistant Attorney General, and Russell C. Wismer, Special Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
